lesson.menu_title: Review & Consolidation 3 -- The Full Team Meeting
lesson.menu_desc: Block 3 Review & Full Meeting Simulation -- 27 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 18 -- Review & Consolidation 3 -- Running a Full Team Meeting
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Rafael de Andrade Brandão | Aula 18 -- Review & Consolidation 3

listenings.text: Hi Rafael, this is Diana. Today is a big day. You will chair the full quarterly team meeting, from start to finish. This is where everything from Block 3 comes together. Let me walk you through the flow, so you feel ready. First, you open the meeting and set the agenda. Say, Let us get started, and walk the team through the points for today. Second, you report the results from last quarter, using the past simple. Say something like, Sales rose by ten percent, and we reached our main goal. Third, you describe the trends. Tell the team what went up, what went down, and what stayed steady. Fourth, you invite input and handle questions. Ask, Are there any questions? and if you are not sure, say, That is a fair point, let me come back to that. Fifth, you close the meeting. Summarize the key takeaway, assign each deliverable to an owner, agree on the next steps, and confirm the status of each task. And finally, after the meeting, you send the minutes and a short follow-up. Remember, a good chair keeps the meeting on track, gives everyone a chance to speak, and ends with clear action items. You have practiced every piece. Now you put them together. Good luck, and send me the minutes when you are done.

Okay, let me record a quick recap of the team meeting before I write the minutes. I chaired the meeting today, and I think it went well. I opened by setting the agenda and walking the team through the three points. Then I reported the results from last quarter. Sales rose by ten percent, and we reached our main goal. After that, I described the trends. The numbers in the south region went up sharply, while the west region stayed steady. Next, I invited input from the team. There were a few good questions, and one was a fair point about the budget, so I said I would come back to it. To close, I summarized the main takeaway, assigned each deliverable to an owner, and we agreed on the next steps. I confirmed the status of every task. Now I just need to write the minutes and send a short follow-up to the team. A clear meeting, on track from start to finish. That is exactly what Diana wanted to see.

extra_audio.text: Rafael chairs the full quarterly team meeting. First, he opens the meeting and sets the agenda for the day. Then he reports the results from last quarter using the past simple. Next, he describes the trends and invites input from the team. After that, he handles the questions and assigns each deliverable to an owner. Finally, he summarizes the key takeaway, agrees on the next steps, and sends the minutes to the team.
